title: endid — Project Instructions
text: R package implementing Engression-Based Distributional (Lee & Wooldridge 2025). Combines panel transformations engression distributional regression estimate ATT, QTE, counterfactual distributions.

title: endid — Project Instructions
text: R/endid.R — Main entry point. Dispatches common-timing (inline) endid_staggered(). R/transform.R — Unit-specific pre-treatment residualization (demean, detrend, demeanq, detrendq). R/fit_engression.R — Core cross-section estimation via engression. R/bootstrap.R — Bootstrap inference ATT QTE. R/staggered.R — Staggered adoption: per-cohort estimation + weighted aggregation. R/methods.R — S3 methods: print, summary, plot. R/endid-package.R — Package-level documentation imports.

title: endid — Project Instructions
code: # Run tests Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'  # Check package Rscript -e 'devtools::check()'  # Regenerate NAMESPACE / man pages Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()'

title: endid — Project Instructions
text: inst/extdata/castle.csv — Castle Doctrine dataset (50 states, 2000–2010, staggered adoption).

title: endid — Project Instructions
text: Engression neural-network-based distributional regression — results stochastic. Tests use generous tolerances. Bootstrap pools replicate-level draws across cohorts proper staggered inference. .data pronoun rlang used ggplot2 aes() pass R CMD check. ggplot2 Suggests (Imports); plot.endid() checks availability.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ao X (2026). endid: Engression-Based Distributional Difference--Differences. R package version 0.1.0, https://xiangao.github.io/endid/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {endid: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences},   author = {Xiang Ao},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://xiangao.github.io/endid/}, }

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences
text: Engression-based distributional difference--differences. endid combines panel transformations Lee Wooldridge (2025) engression distributional regression. output includes: ATT — Average Treatment Effect Treated QTE — Quantile Treatment Effects across outcome distribution Counterfactual distributions via engression sampling common-timing staggered-adoption designs supported.

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences
code: library(endid)  result <- endid(   data = panel_df,   y = "outcome",   ivar = "unit_id",   tvar = "time",   post = "post_treatment",   dvar = "treated",   rolling = "demean" )  print(result) summary(result) plot(result)

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences
code: castle <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "endid")) castle$gvar <- castle$effyear castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA  result <- endid(   data = castle,   y = "lhomicide",   ivar = "sid",   tvar = "year",   gvar = "gvar",   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated" )  print(result) plot(result)

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences
text: Key arguments endid(): rolling — Transformation method ("demean", "detrend", "demeanq", "detrendq") control_group — staggered: "never_treated" "not_yet_treated" aggregate — staggered: "overall", "cohort", "none" nboot — Number bootstrap replications (default: 200) quantiles — Quantiles QTE (default: seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1))

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences
text: Full documentation: https://xiangao.github.io/endid/

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences
text: Lee, S. & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). Distributional Difference--Differences.

title: Aggregate cohort-level results — aggregate_cohorts
text: Computes weighted average ATT QTE across cohorts using n_treated weights. Pools bootstrap vectors proper inference.

title: Aggregate cohort-level results — aggregate_cohorts
text: cohort_results List per-cohort result lists. quantiles Numeric vector quantiles. nboot Number bootstrap replications.

title: Aggregate cohort-level results — aggregate_cohorts
text: List att_overall qte.

what: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
text: Computes ydot (residualised outcome), ydot_postavg (post-treatment average ydot per unit), marks firstpost cross-section.

title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
code: apply_transform(df, y, ivar, tindex, post, rolling, tpost1, season_var = NULL)

title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
text: df Data frame (long format, one row per unit-period) y Outcome column name ivar Unit identifier column name tindex Integer time index column name post Binary post-treatment indicator column name (0=pre, 1=post) rolling Transformation method: "demean", "detrend", "demeanq", "detrendq" tpost1 First post-treatment period index season_var Column name seasonal indicator (required demeanq/detrendq)

title: Apply unit-specific transformation to panel data — apply_transform
text: Data frame added columns: ydot, ydot_postavg, firstpost

title: Bootstrap inference for endid — bootstrap_endid
text: Resamples units (replacement) cross-section, refits engression, computes ATT QTE replicate. Returns SEs percentile CIs. Supports parallel execution via mclapply Unix-like systems.

title: Bootstrap inference for endid — bootstrap_endid
code: bootstrap_endid(   Y,   D,   controls = NULL,   nboot = 200,   quantiles = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1),   nsample = 500,   alpha = 0.05,   noise_dim = 5,   hidden_dim = 100,   num_layer = 3,   num_epochs = 1000,   lr = 0.001,   silent = TRUE,   num_cores = 1 )

title: Bootstrap inference for endid — bootstrap_endid
text: Y Numeric vector residualized outcomes. D Binary treatment indicator. controls Matrix controls NULL. nboot Number bootstrap replications (default: 200). quantiles Quantiles QTE (default: seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)). nsample Monte Carlo samples engression predict (default: 500). alpha Significance level CIs (default: 0.05). noise_dim, hidden_dim, num_layer, num_epochs, lr, silent Engression parameters. num_cores Number cores parallel execution (default: 1).

title: Bootstrap inference for endid — bootstrap_endid
text: List : se, ci_lower, ci_upper, att_boot, att_mean, qte_se, qte_ci_lo, qte_ci_hi, qte_mean, qte_boot_mat.

title: endid: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid-package
text: Combines Lee & Wooldridge (2025) panel transformations engression distributional regression produce quantile treatment effects (QTE), conditional average treatment effects (CATE), counterfactual distributions. Implements unit-specific residualization uses engression nonlinear distributional estimation. Supports common-timing staggered adoption designs bootstrap inference.

title: endid: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid-package
text: Maintainer: Xiang Ao xiangao@example.com

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid
text: Estimates distributional treatment effects combining Lee & Wooldridge (2025) panel transformations engression distributional regression. Produces ATT, quantile treatment effects (QTE), counterfactual distributions.

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid
code: endid(   data,   y,   ivar,   tvar,   gvar = NULL,   post = NULL,   dvar = NULL,   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate = "overall",   controls = NULL,   season_var = NULL,   quantiles = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1),   nsample = 500,   nboot = 200,   noise_dim = 5,   hidden_dim = 100,   num_layer = 3,   num_epochs = 1000,   lr = 0.001,   num_cores = 1,   silent = TRUE )

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid
text: data long-format panel data frame. y Character. Outcome column name. ivar Character. Unit identifier column name. tvar Character. Calendar time column name (numeric). gvar Character NULL. First-treatment-year column staggered designs. Units value 0, NA, Inf never-treated. post Character NULL. Binary post-treatment indicator column (0 = pre, 1 = post). Required gvar = NULL. dvar Character NULL. Binary treatment group indicator column (1 = treated unit, 0 = control). Required common-timing designs post calendar indicator (units observed pre post). rolling Character. Transformation method: "demean" (default), "detrend", "demeanq", "detrendq". control_group Character. Control group staggered designs: "never_treated" (default) "not_yet_treated". aggregate Character. Aggregation staggered designs: "overall" (default), "cohort", "none". controls Character vector NULL. Time-invariant control column names. season_var Character NULL. Seasonal indicator column. quantiles Numeric vector. Quantiles QTE (default: seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)). nsample Integer. Monte Carlo samples engression predictions (default: 500). nboot Integer. Bootstrap replications inference (default: 200). noise_dim Engression noise dimension (default: 5). hidden_dim Engression hidden layer width (default: 100). num_layer Engression number layers (default: 3). num_epochs Engression training epochs (default: 1000). lr Engression learning rate (default: 1e-3). num_cores Integer. Number cores bootstrap parallelization (default: 1). silent Logical. Suppress engression training output (default: TRUE).

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid
text: object class "endid".

title: Engression-Based Distributional Difference-in-Differences — endid
code: # \donttest{   castle <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "endid"))   castle$gvar <- castle$effyear   castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA   res <- endid(castle, "lhomicide", "sid", "year", gvar = "gvar",                rolling = "demean", num_epochs = 500, nboot = 50)   print(res) # }

what: Staggered adoption DiD via engression — endid_staggered
title: Staggered adoption DiD via engression — endid_staggered
text: Estimates cohort-specific ATT QTE staggered treatment designs, aggregates across cohorts following Callaway & Sant'Anna (2021).

title: Staggered adoption DiD via engression — endid_staggered
code: endid_staggered(   data,   y,   ivar,   tvar,   gvar,   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate = "overall",   controls = NULL,   season_var = NULL,   quantiles = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1),   nsample = 500,   nboot = 200,   noise_dim = 5,   hidden_dim = 100,   num_layer = 3,   num_epochs = 1000,   lr = 0.001,   num_cores = 1,   silent = TRUE )

title: Staggered adoption DiD via engression — endid_staggered
text: data Long-format panel data frame. y, ivar, tvar, gvar Column names (character). rolling, control_group, aggregate, controls, season_var See endid. quantiles, nsample, nboot, noise_dim, hidden_dim, num_layer, num_epochs, lr, num_cores, silent Engression / bootstrap parameters.

title: Staggered adoption DiD via engression — endid_staggered
text: endid object design = "staggered".

what: Fit engression on a DiD cross-section — fit_engression_cs
title: Fit engression on a DiD cross-section — fit_engression_cs
text: Core estimation function. Fits engression Y ~ (D, controls) computes ATT QTE comparing predictions D=1 vs D=0.

title: Fit engression on a DiD cross-section — fit_engression_cs
code: fit_engression_cs(   Y,   D,   controls = NULL,   quantiles = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1),   nsample = 500,   noise_dim = 5,   hidden_dim = 100,   num_layer = 3,   num_epochs = 1000,   lr = 0.001,   silent = TRUE )

title: Fit engression on a DiD cross-section — fit_engression_cs
text: Y Numeric vector residualized outcomes (ydot_postavg). D Binary treatment indicator (0/1). controls Matrix control variables NULL. quantiles Numeric vector quantiles QTE (default: seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)). nsample Number Monte Carlo samples engression predictions (default: 500). noise_dim Engression noise dimension (default: 5). hidden_dim Engression hidden layer width (default: 100). num_layer Engression number layers (default: 3). num_epochs Training epochs (default: 1000). lr Learning rate (default: 1e-3). silent Suppress training output (default: TRUE).

title: Fit engression on a DiD cross-section — fit_engression_cs
text: List : model (engression object), att (scalar), qte (data.frame), samples_treated (numeric vector), samples_control (numeric vector).
